title: erophila vulgaris var. muricola
intro: Erophila vulgaris var. muricola, also known as Mouse-ear cress, is a subspecies of the annual herb Erophila vulgaris. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities. The health benefits of Erophila vulgaris var. muricola include its ability to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and stress. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
